title: Quercus gracilis
intro: Quercus gracilis, also known as the Mexican white oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The health benefits of Quercus gracilis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Quercus gracilis include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which have been identified as quercetin and other quercus compounds. The most common herbal preparations made with Quercus gracilis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as extracts used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Quercus gracilis is classified as a species of tree within the genus Quercus and the family Fagaceae. It belongs to the order Fagales and the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is further divided into the subclass Rosidae. Quercus gracilis is a member of the family Fagaceae, commonly known as the beech and oak family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and anatomical characteristics.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.
